params.name: Sloane Astrolabe
params.description:
On Mar 20, 1300 AD, the Sloane Astrolabe was completed by a clockmaker.
The Sloane Astrolabe is made entirely of brass, with a main body consisting of a circular disk about half a meter in diameter and several concentric rings, with a pointer attached to the center of the disk that can be rotated freely. The disk is about one centimeter thick and has an astronomical map engraved on it, with a crescent-shaped pointer pointing to specific stars and markings that include the celestial ecliptic, …
